title: Converting Flexbox to Grid — Card Grid
description: You have a card grid built with Flexbox. Convert it to use CSS Grid so that all cards have equal height and the last row doesn't stretch awkwardly.

starterCode.html:
Card 1
A short description
Card 2
A much longer description that takes more space
Card 3
Short
Card 4
Another description
Card 5
Medium length description here

hint: Replace the flexbox properties with `display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr)); gap: 16px;`

solution.css:
.cards { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr)); gap: 16px; }
.card { background: #f0f0f0; padding: 20px; border-radius: 8px; }

title: Grid Outside, Flexbox Inside
description: Build a 3-column layout (features section) where Grid handles the columns and rows, and Flexbox handles the internal card layout (icon, title, description, button).

starterCode.html:
🎨

Beautiful, modern interfaces designed with user experience as the top priority.

⚡

Performance

Lightning-fast load times with optimized code and modern best practices.

🔒

Security

Enterprise-grade security with encryption and regular audits.

hint: Grid: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr))`. Card: `display: flex; flex-direction: column;`. Button: `margin-top: auto;` to push it to the bottom.

title: Build a Dashboard Using Both
description: Build a dashboard page that uses Grid for the overall layout and Flexbox for component internals. The dashboard has: header, sidebar nav, main content panel (with stats cards in a Grid), and a right sidebar with a Flexbox-based feed.

hint: Header: `display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;`. Stats grid: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr))`. Sidebar: `display: flex; flex-direction: column;`. Feed: `display: flex; flex-direction: column;`.
